reviews.review_description:
Seriously, stop reading this review and buy the game already! You won't regret it. The game is difficult, but that's because space flight is difficult. 

Tip: Quit trying to turn yourself right side up. Up and down don’t exist in space.

This game looks great. Love the soundtrack too. The controls are far too unforgiving for casual gaming. Gameplay requires a lot of patience and precision but, the reward is complete satisfaction and  total immersion. Good job by the developer(s). Can't wait to see what's next.

It's got some rough edges, but it's actively under development. It was exactly the kind of thing I was hoping for when I bought a VR headset. There were a couple bugs when I first got the game, but the developer has rolled out updates as soon as he was made aware of them. Kudos. Keep up the good work

Love the flight mechanics, its challanging! Great sim!
Had an issue with sound cutting out but i reinstalled and disabled Viper4Ansroid and all is working perfectly now. 

Thanks for the great game dev:)

I'm a big fan of this game. The control scheme takes a lot to get used to, but that's not to say I don't like it. The game is very difficult even on normal, which I appreciate since most games on the Gear VR are far too easy to play. Most games can also be beaten fairly quickly which is absolutely not the case with this one, it seems like it'll provide hours of entertainment, which at $2 is a steal. I highly recommend this to anyone that likes flying games like End Space and anyone that has a lot of patience. Like I said the controls take a lot of time to get used to and it's quite difficult so it's definitely not for everyone, but I love it! :D

A bit confusing to start, as there are no menu items to choose from, and my controller doesn't have a start button. But it's very worth the trial and error to get it going. I admit, though, I was getting really close to trying to get my money back.  Very fun, very challenging, very cool spacecraft control and reaction. Haven't tried turning off the automatic slowing of the craft. Amazing realism in its behavior. Thanks!

So far it's my favorite game for the Gear VR. Controls are responsive, and the physics are good. Works well with the Steel Series controller. The biggest flaw in the game is that the map is heavily lacking in information. It's hard to tell which way you''re facing, and there's no points of refferance. I would like if it showed where the locked doors and portals are, and which sectors are which. I spent several hours going in circles trying to find the yellow keycard in the third or fourth area of Chapter 1 and had to quit after realising I was completely lost.
